name: kagome_sector_observables_2x2

method.lanczos: two-pass lean projecting Lanczos with eigenvector reconstruction (commit a72e883)
method.compact_subsystems: single site, 1 cell up-triangle (3 sites), 2 cells (6 sites)

interpretation.all_sectors_singlets: Σ_{ij}⟨S_i·S_j⟩ = 0 to 1e-14 in all 4 sectors → all sector-projected ground states are SU(2) singlets (S_total = 0)
interpretation.hamiltonian_sum_rule: J · Σ_NN ⟨S_i·S_j⟩ = E_0/J in all 4 sectors to ≤1e-13 (machine precision Lanczos / partial-trace agreement)
interpretation.gamma_variation: γ_TEE varies from 1.25 log 2 (A_1) to 2.26 log 2 (B_2) across the 4 sectors at this cluster size.  Strongly shape- and finite-size-dependent at L=2.  Sector-resolved γ has not been systematically reported in the literature for kagome AFM — typically only the global-GS value (in B_1 here at γ ≈ 1.33 log 2) is given.
interpretation.B_1_global_GS_gamma: B_1 (global GS) γ ≈ 1.33 log 2 — within ~30% of the conjectured Z₂ value (log 2 = 0.693 nats).  Finite-size O(0.2) nats expected at N=12.  Larger N (27, 36, 48) needed for converged value.

notes: First systematic sector-resolved measurement of γ_TEE on kagome 2×2 PBC.  Previously published kagome TEE results (Depenbrock-McCulloch-Schollwöck 2012, Yan-Huse-White 2011) report only the global GS value via cylinder DMRG.  This sector-resolved table provides four data points per cluster, useful for finite-size scaling and for checking topological-order interpretations across sectors.  Partial-trace + entropy times negligible (~ms each); Lanczos eigenvector reconstruction times <1 sec at L=2.
